prevalence.value: ~0.05-0.2% (SCD; higher in tribal/central India); trait common in some regions
prevalence.source: Indian hemoglobinopathy program syntheses; public health reports and cohort studies
prevalence.trend: stable births; improved survival increases prevalent population

incidence.value: High in hotspot states; newborn/antenatal screening programs expanding
incidence.source: Hemoglobinopathy screening program reports and state health initiatives

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate in affected regions due to high prevalence; high per-patient morbidity
burden.mortalityRate: Elevated without early comprehensive care; reduced with prophylaxis and hydroxyurea
burden.source: IHME/GBD hemoglobinopathies context; Indian cohort outcomes

riskFactors: limited access to early prophylaxis and comprehensive care
vulnerablePopulations: patients with recurrent pain crises and anemia
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Screening (newborn/antenatal where available) plus confirmatory hemoglobin electrophoresis/HPLC; ongoing monitoring for stroke, renal and pulmonary complications

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; expanding in high-burden states, limited nationally
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands to >1 million (varies by estimates; underdiagnosed)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improves with structured comprehensive care and hydroxyurea access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: State screening programs in hotspots; confirmatory testing availability varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Vaccination and infection prophylaxis, hydroxyurea, transfusion protocols for complications, pain management, stroke prevention (TCD where available), community-based follow-up
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access barriers in rural/tribal areas, stigma, limited specialist follow-up, inconsistent availability of comprehensive pain management
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Hydroxyurea is affordable; targeted public programs in high-burden states are expanding
